name: Kwanzaa
description: Seven principles, seven candles, seven days — the African-American cultural celebration created in 1966.

questions.q: Who created Kwanzaa?

source.notes: Karenga is a professor at California State University, Long Beach.

questions.q: In what year was Kwanzaa first celebrated?

source.notes: Created in the wake of the 1965 Watts riots in Los Angeles.

questions.q: What dates does Kwanzaa cover each year?
From which African language does the name 'Kwanzaa' come?

source.notes: From the Swahili phrase 'matunda ya kwanza' = 'first fruits'.

questions.q: What does the Swahili phrase 'matunda ya kwanza' mean?

source.notes: Inspired by African first-fruits harvest festivals.

questions.q: How many candles are placed on the Kinara during Kwanzaa?

source.notes: One for each day / principle. The kinara holds three red, one black (center), and three green.

questions.q: What three colors are central to Kwanzaa decorations?

source.notes: The same colors as the Pan-African flag.

questions.q: What is the Swahili name for Kwanzaa's seven guiding principles?
Which principle, celebrated on Day 1 of Kwanzaa, means 'unity'?

source.notes: Unity in family, community, nation, and race.

questions.q: Which Kwanzaa principle means 'self-determination'?

source.notes: To define ourselves, name ourselves, create for ourselves, speak for ourselves.

questions.q: Which Kwanzaa principle means 'collective work and responsibility'?

source.notes: Building and maintaining the community together.

questions.q: Which Kwanzaa principle means 'cooperative economics'?

source.notes: Building and supporting Black-owned businesses and shared prosperity.

questions.q: What does the Kwanzaa principle 'Nia' mean?

source.notes: Building community to restore people to their traditional greatness.

questions.q: What does the Kwanzaa principle 'Kuumba' mean?

source.notes: To leave the community more beautiful and beneficial than it was.

questions.q: What does the Kwanzaa principle 'Imani' mean?

source.notes: Faith in the people, parents, teachers, leaders, and the righteousness of the struggle.

questions.q: What is the name of the celebratory feast held on the sixth night of Kwanzaa (December 31)?
What greeting do people exchange during Kwanzaa, meaning 'What is the news?' in Swahili?

source.notes: The reply is the principle of the day (Umoja, Kujichagulia, etc.).

questions.q: Which U.S. president was the first to issue an official Kwanzaa greeting from the White House?

source.notes: First official greeting in 1997. Every subsequent president has done so as well.

questions.q: Which African festival traditions chiefly inspired Kwanzaa?

source.notes: Notably the Zulu Umkhosi Wokweshwama and Ashanti / Yoruba first-fruits ceremonies.

questions.q: What U.S. event most directly motivated Karenga's creation of Kwanzaa as a cultural-unity holiday?
choices: The 1965 Watts riots in Los Angeles

source.notes: Karenga sought to create a holiday that would 'give Blacks an alternative to the existing holiday and give Blacks an opportunity to celebrate themselves and their history.'
